{"step_by_step_thinking": "Pilocarpine acts on the sphincter pupillae to cause miosis (pupil constriction). To find a drug that acts on the dilator pupillae with an analogous action to pilocarpine, we need to look for a drug that causes pupil dilation. Among the options, only tropicamide is described as a mydriatic (pupil dilator) in the documents. Tropicamide acts on the dilator pupillae to cause pupil dilation, making it the drug with an analogous action to pilocarpine on the sphincter pupillae.", "answer_choice": "D"}
